title: cordyla africana
intro: Cordyla africana, also known as the tree gum or taheebo, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia and Malaysia. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Cordyla africana include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, as well as its potential to prevent certain types of cancer. Cordyla africana is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
